skill.name: maxhub-hybrid
skill.description: HybridParser multi-platform content parsing and aggregation skill for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Extracts and aggregates content data from multiple video platforms via MaxHub API.
skill.summary: Multi-platform content parsing and aggregation via MaxHub API
skill.author: MaxHub Team

security_audit.summary: After AI review, the static findings are predominantly FALSE POSITIVES. The skill is a legitimate API wrapper for content parsing with clear security documentation. All 'external_commands' detections are markdown code fences, not actual command execution. Environment variable access is documented and restricted to user-configured API credentials. Network access is limited to HTTPS calls to MaxHub API only. The DANGEROUS_COMBINATION heuristic flagged code examples but these are documentation samples, not executable code.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for API Authentication
low_findings.description: Skill accesses environment variables MAXHUB_API_KEY and MAXHUB_BASE_URL for API authentication. This is legitimate configuration documented in frontmatter. No hardcoded credentials found. Confidence: 0.85 - These are documented environment variable names, not actual secret values.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The env_access detections are for documented configuration variables MAXHUB_API_KEY and MAXHUB_BASE_URL. These are standard environment variable names used for user-configured authentication, not hardcoded secrets.

low_findings.title: Network Access to External API Service
low_findings.description: Skill makes HTTPS requests to MaxHub API service (aconfig.cn). Access is documented and restricted per security metadata. Confidence: 0.90 - Clear documentation shows network access limited to single API service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network access is documented in SKILL.md as restricted to MAXHUB_BASE_URL only. The URLs are legitimate service endpoints for the MaxHub API platform.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Detected as Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner detected 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' at multiple locations in SKILL.md and api-catalog.md. These are FALSE POSITIVES - the backticks are markdown code fence syntax (```) used for documentation examples showing curl commands, not actual command execution. The skill is documentation-only with no executable code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The backticks in markdown code fences are clearly documentation syntax for code examples. No actual Ruby or shell execution occurs - only documentation showing how to use curl.

content.user_title: Parse Multi-Platform Video Content via MaxHub API
content.value_statement: Users need to extract video content data from multiple platforms but lack a unified API. This skill provides a single interface to parse and aggregate video data from various sources through the MaxHub API.
actual_capabilities: Parse video content from multiple platforms via MaxHub API
Extract video metadata including title, author, and engagement metrics
Chain multiple API calls for complex data retrieval scenarios
Handle authentication via environment-configured API keys
Process rate-limited API requests with automatic pagination
Aggregate data from different sources into unified output
limitations: Requires valid MaxHub API key with sufficient credits
Only supports platforms available through MaxHub API
Cannot access private or paywalled content
Rate limited to 60 calls per minute by default

use_cases.title: Research Video Trending Topics
use_cases.description: Extract and analyze trending video content across platforms to identify emerging topics and trends.

use_cases.title: Aggregate Creator Statistics
use_cases.description: Collect performance metrics for multiple creators across different video platforms.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and talent scouts

use_cases.title: Build Content Datasets
use_cases.description: Gather large datasets of video metadata for machine learning training or academic research.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Video Parsing
prompt_templates.prompt: Parse this video URL and extract the metadata: {video_url}
prompt_templates.scenario: When user provides a direct video link

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Platform Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for videos about {topic} across platforms using MaxHub and summarize the top results
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants to search across multiple platforms

prompt_templates.title: Creator Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the content statistics for creator {creator_id} on multiple platforms and compare their performance
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants cross-platform creator analytics

prompt_templates.title: Batch Data Collection
prompt_templates.prompt: Collect video metadata for the top 10 trending videos about {keyword}, including engagement metrics and publish dates
prompt_templates.scenario: When user needs bulk data collection

best_practices: Always check your MaxHub API balance before running batch operations
Configure MAXHUB_BASE_URL correctly for your region (default: https://www.aconfig.cn)
Use pagination limits to avoid unexpected API costs during large queries
anti_patterns: Do not run unbounded batch queries without setting result limits
Do not share your MAXHUB_API_KEY in public channels or repositories
Do not attempt to access content behind authentication without proper permissions

faq.question: What platforms does this skill support?
faq.answer: Supported platforms depend on MaxHub API coverage. The skill focuses on video content platforms including Douyin and similar services.

faq.question: How do I get a MaxHub API key?
faq.answer: Register at https://www.aconfig.cn, create an account, and generate an API key from your dashboard.

faq.question: Is there a rate limit?
faq.answer: Yes, MaxHub API defaults to 60 requests per minute. The skill includes built-in rate limit handling.

faq.question: Can I access private videos?
faq.answer: No, this skill only accesses publicly available data through the MaxHub API.

faq.question: How much does it cost to use?
faq.answer: MaxHub API charges per request. Check your account balance and set cost alert thresholds in the skill settings.

faq.question: Is my API key stored or shared?
faq.answer: No, your API key is passed only through environment variables and never stored, logged, or shared.
